up.method: carbon-source consumption rate reported as bar chart in Fig 2 (no numeric value in text)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Entner-Doudoroff (ED-like) route fraction of fructose catabolism
Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas (EMP-like) route fraction of fructose catabolism
mfa.note: from [1-13C]fructose label tracking into ectoines; ED 81% + EMP 19% = 100%

yields.Yxs: reported in Fig 2 as Y_X/Fru bar chart; higher biomass yield on fructose than glucose except at 2.5 M NaCl (no numeric value in text)

growth_records.cite: Pastor JM et al. Fructose metabolism in Chromohalobacter salexigens: interplay between the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas and Entner-Doudoroff pathways. Microbial Cell Factories (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12934-019-1178-x. [PMC6692947]
growth_records.snippet: Tracking isotopic label from [1-13C] fructose to ectoines revealed that 81% and 19% of the fructose were metabolized through ED and EMP-like routes, respectively. ... Pyruvate and acetate were not detected in fructose culture supernatants.
growth_records.notes: 13C-MFA-style flux split: ED 81% / EMP 19% of fructose, measured by 13C-NMR label tracking of [1-13C]fructose into ectoine/hydroxyectoine at 0.75 M NaCl. Fructose grown cells showed NO carbon overflow (pyruvate and acetate not detected) -> secretion rate = 0. Specific growth rate (Fig 1a) and biomass yield/consumption rate (Fig 1b, Fig 2) are reported only as bar charts; no numeric mu, Yxs or qS values appear in the text, so those fields are null. Text states fructose growth rate is 30-40% lower than glucose at all salinities. Intracellular ectoine content at 2.5 M NaCl = 1.419 +/- 0.087 mmol/gCDW (fructose) reported separately.

up.method: consumption rate reported as bar chart (Fig 2); no numeric value in text

yields.Yxs: Fig 2 bar chart (no numeric text value); biomass yield on fructose similar to glucose at 2.5 M NaCl

growth_records.cite: Pastor JM et al. Fructose metabolism in Chromohalobacter salexigens: interplay between the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas and Entner-Doudoroff pathways. Microbial Cell Factories (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12934-019-1178-x. [PMC6692947]
growth_records.snippet: intracellular ectoines accumulation was higher in the fructose-grown cultures (1.419 +/- 0.087 mmol/gCDW) as compared to glucose grown cultures (1.195 +/- 0.037 mmol/gCDW).
growth_records.notes: High-salinity fructose batch. Intracellular ectoines (ectoine+hydroxyectoine) = 1.419 +/- 0.087 mmol/gCDW (an intracellular pool, not a secretion rate; placed in product_yields). No pyruvate/acetate overflow. Growth rate and yields are figure-only (no numeric text values). Salinity 1.5 M NaCl (87.6 g/L) is a third assayed fructose condition with intermediate behaviour, not separately quantified in text.

up.method: consumption rate reported as bar chart (Fig 2), higher than fructose; no numeric value in text

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Entner-Doudoroff route fraction of glucose catabolism
mfa.note: glucose metabolized exclusively through ED pathway in C. salexigens

yields.Yxs: Fig 2 (no numeric text value); biomass yield increases with salinity, lower than fructose at 0.75 M NaCl

growth_records.cite: Pastor JM et al. Fructose metabolism in Chromohalobacter salexigens: interplay between the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas and Entner-Doudoroff pathways. Microbial Cell Factories (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12934-019-1178-x. [PMC6692947]
growth_records.snippet: While glucose is metabolized exclusively through the Entner-Doudoroff (ED) pathway ... high pyruvate and acetate overflow at low salinity.
growth_records.notes: Glucose at 0.75 M NaCl catabolized 100% via ED (0% EMP). Carbon overflow (pyruvate + acetate secreted) at low salinity is reported here and characterized quantitatively in the companion paper ref [11] (Pastor et al.; not this study) - so secretion rates are is_experimental true but numeric values are NOT in this paper. Growth rate/yield are figure-only.

up.method: consumption rate reported as bar chart (Fig 2); no numeric value in text

sec.method: carbon overflow very low at high salinity
carbon overflow very low at high salinity

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Entner-Doudoroff route fraction of glucose catabolism

yields.Yxs: Fig 2 (no numeric text value); maximum biomass yield at 2.5 M NaCl similar to fructose

growth_records.cite: Pastor JM et al. Fructose metabolism in Chromohalobacter salexigens: interplay between the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas and Entner-Doudoroff pathways. Microbial Cell Factories (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12934-019-1178-x. [PMC6692947]
growth_records.snippet: glucose grown cultures (1.195 +/- 0.037 mmol/gCDW) ... at 2.5 M NaCl, where both were similar ... carbon overflow in glucose cultures was very low.
growth_records.notes: High-salinity glucose batch. Intracellular ectoines = 1.195 +/- 0.037 mmol/gCDW (intracellular pool, product_yields). Carbon overflow very low at 2.5 M NaCl. Growth rate/yield figure-only. Glucose exclusively ED.

growth_records.medium: chemically defined medium, glucose as sole carbon source

up.method: experimental batch cultivation (values cited from Fallet et al. 2010, ref 70); range of measured specific glucose uptake rates at varying glucose concentrations, used to validate model growth flux (in silico vs experimental growth agreed within 1.5-2.5% error for 3.307, 3.478 and 3.751 mmol/gDW/h)

sec.method: experimental batch cultivation result reported by Fallet et al. 2010 (ref 70), glucose as sole carbon source; the paper's own FBA at 1.5 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake predicted 0.75 mmol/gDW/h ectoine, compared against this experimental 0.72

product_yields.ectoine_on_glucose_in_silico: 49-50% mmol ectoine/mmol glucose (FBA-predicted range at 3-10 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake; not experimental)

growth_records.cite: Ates, Ozlem et al. Genome-scale reconstruction of metabolic network for a halophilic extremophile, Chromohalobacter salexigens DSM 3043. BMC Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-5-12. [PMC3034673]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental and in silico growth fluxes for batch cultivation of C. salexigens at varying glucose uptake rates (3.193 - 3.751 mmol/gDW/h)... The performed FBA simulations with 1.5 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake resulted in an ectoine production
growth_records.notes: This is a genome-scale reconstruction (iOA584) paper; nearly all rates in it are FBA/in silico predictions, NOT measured here. The only EXPERIMENTAL prokaryote rates are borrowed from reference 70 = Fallet C, Rohe P, Franco-Lara E, 'Process optimization of the integrated synthesis and secretion of ectoine and hydroxyectoine under hyper/hypo-osmotic stress', Biotechnol Bioeng, 2010. Cited experimental values: (a) measured specific glucose uptake rates for batch cultivation spanning 3.193, 3.307, 3.478, 3.751 mmol/gDW/h (individual per-concentration growth rates are shown only in Figure 3, not given numerically in the text); (b) experimental ectoine production of 0.72 mmol/gDW/h with glucose as sole carbon source. Medium described only as 'chemically defined media with glucose as sole carbon source'; no numeric composition, temperature, or pH given here. In-silico-only figures deliberately EXCLUDED: e.g. isoleucine 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.129 h-1; fructose 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.1290 h-1; sucrose 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.0646 h-1; ectoine yield 49-50% and biomass 0.1934-0.642 h-1 over 3-10 mmol/gDW/h glucose; choline-stimulation growth 0.065->0.129 h-1 -- all FBA predictions, not experimental.
